description: Track delivery of the TypeWeaver roadmap from scoring model through adversarial optimization. Source docs: docs/openspec-implementation-roadmap.md, docs/openspec-product.md, docs/openspec-scoring-model.md, docs/openspec-attack-matrix.md, docs/openspec-demo-flow.md.
notes: Use the roadmap milestone order. Favor evaluation-first, then repair, then generation, then closed-loop optimization.
title: Implement TypeWeaver product loop from OpenSpec roadmap

acceptance_criteria: Stable JSON score contract exists; at least one preset weighting scheme implemented; score output is understandable in UI/API contexts; benchmark output can emit the new schema.
description: Create the first stable scoring schema and weighting model for TypeWeaver. Include overall score, robustness, distinctiveness, cost, condition breakdown, worst-case score, and top failure summary. Align implementation with docs/openspec-scoring-model.md.
title: Define and implement scoring model v1

acceptance_criteria: A single font can be evaluated reproducibly across the v1 matrix; outputs feed the scoring system; worst-case condition and collapse threshold are identifiable.
description: Implement the first hostile-condition matrix for TypeWeaver using deterministic primitives and severity ladders. Start with baseline, blur, low contrast, small size, raster degradation, compression, and confusion-pair probes. Align implementation with docs/openspec-attack-matrix.md.
title: Implement deterministic attack matrix v1

acceptance_criteria: User can select a font, choose a scenario, see multiple condition previews, inspect score breakdowns, and identify worst-case conditions in a live demo.
description: Build the first demoable browser experience for TypeWeaver. Users should be able to upload or select a font, choose a scenario, inspect hostile-condition permutations, and view score changes and failure hotspots. Align implementation with docs/openspec-demo-flow.md.
title: Build permutation explorer UI

acceptance_criteria: Starting from one font, TypeWeaver can produce multiple repair variants, compare them side by side, and explain the tradeoffs in robustness, worst-case performance, and cost.
description: Create the first repair/mutation workflow for existing fonts. Generate several candidate variants, score them under the same matrix, rank them against the original, and explain what changed.
title: Generate and rank repair variants

acceptance_criteria: User can choose a winning candidate, inspect tradeoff summaries, and download a font variant plus supporting report/provenance artifacts.
description: Add ranked comparison and export capabilities so the product produces usable outcomes. Support original-vs-variant comparison, tradeoff sorting, and downloadable font/report artifacts.
title: Add comparison frontier and export flow

acceptance_criteria: User can provide intent and receive multiple candidates that are scored under target conditions and compared on robustness, vibe fit, and weight.
description: Support generation of candidate fonts from vibe, constraints, and references. Generated outputs must be evaluated under the same score and attack systems as imported or repaired fonts.
title: Implement vibe-driven font generation

acceptance_criteria: The system can iterate across multiple generations automatically, show optimization progress, and present a tradeoff frontier rather than a single opaque winner.
description: Automate the attack → mutate → rescore → rank loop. Add adaptive attack selection, mutation policies informed by failure modes, stopping rules, and frontier visualization.
title: Implement adversarial optimization loop
